ogTitle: Paxton vs Paynes Crossing: Climate Safety

heading: Paxton vs Paynes Crossing — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Paxton, New South Wales and Paynes Crossing, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Paynes Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Paxton (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Paxton or Paynes Crossing safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Paxton has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Paynes Crossing scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Paxton scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Paynes Crossing scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Paxton or Paynes Crossing?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Paynes Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.78% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
